action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|......B..|
 2|..#..###.|
 3|.........|
 4|#q.......|
 5|..#......|
 6|#xp.Sy#..|
 7|.#.#..#..|
 8|......#..|
 9|...12.A..|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (5,6) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (5,6), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (7,9): open exactly when (t mod 4) is in {0, 1}. So it is open at t=0,1,4,5,... and closed at t=2,3,6,7,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (7,1): open exactly when (t mod 4) is in {1, 2}. So it is open at t=1,2,5,6,... and closed at t=0,3,4,7,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (3,6); t mod 4=1: (2,6); t mod 4=2: (2,5); t mod 4=3: (2,6). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (2,4); t mod 8=1: (3,4); t mod 8=2: (3,3); t mod 8=3: (2,3); t mod 8=4: (2,2); t mod 8=5: (2,3); t mod 8=6: (3,3); t mod 8=7: (3,4). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (4,9) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (2,6) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (5,9) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (6,6) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 25 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 5 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS:
I need to deliver Parcel 1 (pickup at 4,9; deliver at 2,6) and Parcel 2 (pickup at 5,9; deliver at 6,6).
Start at (5,6) at t=0.
Parcel 1 starts at (4,9). To get there from (5,6), I need to go West to (4,6), then North to (4,9).
Gate B is at (7,1) and is open at t=1, 2, 5, 6... (t mod 4 in {1,2}). This is irrelevant for the path to (4,9) as I am moving West and North, staying away from column 7.
Guard P cycle: (3,6) at t=0, (2,6) at t=1, (2,5) at t=2, (2,6) at t=3.
At t=3, Guard P is at (2,6). This is the delivery spot for Parcel 1.
If I arrive at (2,6) at t=3, I will be caught by Guard P before I can pick up Parcel 2 later or deliver Parcel 1 *after* being caught? Wait, the rules say: "If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell".
So if I arrive at the delivery cell (2,6) at t=3, I deliver Parcel 1. But at the same moment (end of tick), Guard P is at (2,6). So I get caught. The game resets to start (5,6). This is bad. I cannot arrive at (2,6) at t=3.
I must avoid (2,6) at t=3.
Guard P is also at (2,6) at t=1. So I cannot arrive at (2,6) at t=1 either.
Guard P is at (2,5) at t=2.
Guard P path: (3,6)->(2,6)->(2,5)->(2,6)...
Safe times to be at (2,6): t=0 (I am at 5,6, not 2,6), t=4, t=5, t=6, t=7...
I need to get to (4,9) first.
Path to (4,9) from (5,6):
(5,6) -> W -> (4,6) [t=1]. Check Gate B? No, Gate B is at (7,1). (4,6) is safe. Check Guards? P is at (3,6) at t=1. Safe. Q is at (3,4) at t=1. Safe.
(4,6) -> N -> (4,7) [t=2]. Check Guards? P is at (2,5). Safe. Q is at (3,3). Safe.
(4,7) -> N -> (4,8) [t=3]. Check Guards? P is at (2,6). Safe. Q is at (2,3). Safe.
(4,8) -> N -> (4,9) [t=4]. Check Guards? P is at (2,6) at t=4 (cycle repeats every 4: 0,1,2,3,0... so t=4 is same as t=0). P is at (3,6). Safe. Q is at (2,4) at t=4. Safe.
So at t=4, I am at (4,9). I pick up Parcel 1.
Now I have Parcel 1. I need to deliver it to (2,6).
Current pos: (4,9) at t=4.
Target: (2,6).
I need to get to (2,6) by t=4 + k.
But I cannot be at (2,6) at t=4 (Guard P is there).
Cannot be at (2,6) at t=5 (Guard P is at (2,6) at t=5? Cycle: 0:(3,6), 1:(2,6), 2:(2,5), 3:(2,6). t=5 is same as t=1. So P is at (2,6) at t=5).
Cannot be at (2,6) at t=6 (P is at (2,5)). Safe from P.
Cannot be at (2,6) at t=7 (P is at (2,6)). Bad.
So I must
action.label: response

action.content:
Your last response could not be processed: missing or malformed POSITION line.
Answer again, using exactly this format (and nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS:
I need to deliver Parcel 1 (pickup at 4,9; deliver at 2,6) and Parcel 2 (pickup at 5,9; deliver at 6,6).
Start at (5,6) at t=0.
First, I must gather both parcels.
Parcel 1 is at (4,9). From (5,6), I can move West to (4,6) then North to (4,9).
Path: (5,6) -> W -> (4,6) [t=1]. No gate issues. Guard P is at (3,6) [t=1], Guard Q is at (3,4) [t=1]. Safe.
(4,6) -> N -> (4,7) [t=2]. Guard P is at (2,5) [t=2], Guard Q is at (3,3) [t=2]. Safe.
(4,7) -> N -> (4,8) [t=3]. Guard P is at (2,6) [t=3], Guard Q is at (2,3) [t=3]. Safe.
(4,8) -> N -> (4,9) [t=4]. Guard P is at (3,6) [t=4], Guard Q is at (2,4) [t=4]. Safe.
At t=4, I arrive at (4,9), pick up Parcel 1.
Now I have Parcel 1. I need to deliver it to (2,6).
From (4,9) at t=4, I need to reach (2,6).
Path options: West to (3,9), then North to (3,6)? Or West to (3,9), South to (3,8)...?
Guard P is at (3,6) at t=4. If I move W to (3,9) at t=5, I am safe. But at t=6, P moves to (2,6). If I am at (3,6) at t=6, I get caught.
Wait, let's re-evaluate Guard P positions relative to time:
t=0: (3,6)
t=1: (2,6)
t=2: (2,5)
t=3: (2,6)
t=4: (3,6)
t=5: (2,6)
t=6: (2,5)
t=7: (2,6)
...
I need to arrive at (2,6) at a time when P is NOT at (2,6).
P is at (2,6) for t in {1, 3, 5, 7}.
P is at (2,5) for t in {2, 6}.
P is at (3,6) for t in {0, 4, 8}.
So safe times to be at (2,6) are t even? No, t=0 (start), t=2, t=4, t=8...
Wait, P is at (2,6) when t mod 4 is 1 or 3.
So I can be at (2,6) when t mod 4 is 0 or 2.
I am at (4,9) at t=4.
Plan from Plan 1 end (t=4):
Move W to (3,9) [t=5]. Safe (P is at 2,6, I am at 3,9).
Move W to (2,9) [t=6]. Safe (P is at 2,5, I am at 2,9).
Move N to (2,8) [t=7]. Safe? At t=7, P is at (2,6). I am at (2,8). Safe.
Move N to (2,7) [t=8]. At t=8, P is at (3,6) (8 mod 4 = 0). Safe.
Move N to (2,6) [t=9]. At t=9, P is at (2,6) (9 mod 4 = 1). **BAD**. I will be caught.
So I cannot arrive at (2,6) at t=9.
I need to arrive at (2,6) at t=2, 4, 6, 8...
Current timeline:
t=0: Start (5,6).
t=1: W to (4,6).
t=2: N to (4,7).
action.label: response
